summary: This brief post links to Cathy O'Neil's Bloomberg column arguing that COVID-19 contact tracing apps will be ineffective. The core critique is that such technological solutions fail to protect the most vulnerable populations. O'Neil positions this as part of her broader skeptical analysis of tech solutionism during the pandemic.
key_points: COVID-19 tracking apps are presented as ineffective public health tools
The technology fails to address the needs of those most at risk from the virus
This reflects O'Neil's consistent skepticism of algorithmic solutions to social problems
money_quotes: The Covid-19 Tracking App Won't Work
It does nothing for the people who are most at risk
key_insight: Contact tracing technology, like many algorithmic solutions, fails precisely where it matters most—protecting vulnerable populations who often lack access to the technology itself.
